summary: Tested LocalVibe multi-city feature end-to-end. Backend: 15/15 pytest cases pass on auth, multi-city provider listing, filter (Goa→2, Gurgaon→4, Tokyo→0, case-insensitive), featured by city, meta endpoints, provider creation with custom cities, validation of empty cities, role/auth guards. Frontend: login (prefilled demo creds), discover screen (greeting + city pill + 6 categories grid + trending carousel with multi-city '+N' format), city picker bottom-sheet with searchable input + 'Use "<typed>"' custom city option, custom-city filter applies to trending (Goa→2 providers shown), search tab free-text city input + popular-city chip quick-fill (Mumbai→4 results), provider detail page renders chips for all cities a provider serves (verified Sunehri Cakes shows Delhi + Gurgaon chips). One blocking bug found and fixed: index.tsx imported neither KeyboardAvoidingView nor Platform but referenced both — caused runtime crash when opening city picker.

react_native_issues.issue: FIXED by testing agent: missing imports for KeyboardAvoidingView and Platform from react-native caused ReferenceError on city pill tap. Added imports.

action_items: No outstanding action items required from main agent. The KeyboardAvoidingView/Platform import fix was applied by the testing agent and verified.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated FastAPI on_event hooks ('startup'/'shutdown') — consider migrating to lifespan context manager (non-blocking).
CORS allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True is technically invalid per spec; browsers ignore credentials with wildcard. Restrict origins for production.
Provider creation does not generate IDs idempotently for re-seeding; seed only runs when collection is empty (acceptable for dev).
JWT secret loaded from env (good); password hashed with bcrypt $2b$ (good).
seed_data_creation: TestProviderCreation creates TEST_-prefixed provider profiles with random TESTCity<hex> custom cities. These remain in DB but are clearly identifiable and don't affect public city filters because city names are unique per run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use demo@localvibe.app / Demo@12345 (seeker, prefilled) and admin@localvibe.app / Admin@12345. Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_localvibe_api.py -v. Frontend tests use mobile viewport 390x844. Note: Metro is in CI mode (reloads disabled); after any frontend code change, run `sudo supervisorctl restart expo` to pick up changes. Provider 'Plan With Pari' serves 4 cities (Delhi, Jaipur, Udaipur, Goa); 'Frame & Feel Studios' serves Mumbai/Pune/Goa — useful fixtures for multi-city assertions.
rca_of_issue: Bug: City picker bottom sheet crashed with 'KeyboardAvoidingView is not defined'. Reproduction: login → tap city pill on Discover. RCA: index.tsx CityPickerModal used <KeyboardAvoidingView behavior={Platform.OS === 'ios' ? 'padding' : undefined}> but the react-native import statement omitted both KeyboardAvoidingView and Platform. Mitigation: Added both names to the existing react-native named import. Verified by full Playwright run after `supervisorctl restart expo` — city picker opens, accepts input, applies custom-city filter (Goa) and trending list correctly filters to 2 Goa providers.
